ngs_description: Injections (subcutaneous, intramuscular and for varicose veins) and immunizations regularly performed by nursing personnel. In some provinces, these are treated as separate services while in others, they are included in visit fees. Not included are intra-vascular injections performed by physicians and lumbar myelograms which are included in diagnostic/therapeutic services.
raw_text: 115.000 Injections and Immunizations || Injections (subcutaneous, intramuscular and for varicose veins) and immunizations regularly performed by nursing personnel. In some provinces, these are treated as separate services while in others, they are included in visit fees. Not included are intra-vascular injections performed by physicians and lumbar myelograms which are included in diagnostic/therapeutic services.

ngs_description: These fees, typically based on an hourly/weekend rate, are paid for the delivery of specific services (not an individual specific service). For example, many emergency departments now offer physicians a guaranteed sessional fee for working as the doctor on duty, regardless of the number of patients seen.
raw_text: 118.000 Sessional || These fees, typically based on an hourly/weekend rate, are paid for the delivery of specific services (not an individual specific service). For example, many emergency departments now offer physicians a guaranteed sessional fee for working as the doctor on duty, regardless of the number of patients seen.

ngs_description: These are services that are not listed as fee items by a majority of the provinces. For example, sessional and standby fees (where identified by a fee code), mileage, telephone consultations, sexual assault exam for investigation and/or confirmation of alleged sexual assault and other services.
raw_text: 119.000 Other Identified || These are services that are not listed as fee items by a majority of the provinces. For example, sessional and standby fees (where identified by a fee code), mileage, telephone consultations, sexual assault exam for investigation and/or confirmation of alleged sexual assault and other services.

ngs_description: Most provinces coding errors or list codes which are unidentifiable. Includes all services that are unidentified or are identified but paid for by Social Services, the Attorney General, Workers’ Compensation, etc. Examples include services relative to impaired driving, to rape victims and as a result of injuries sustained at work.
raw_text: 120.000 Unidentified || Most provinces coding errors or list codes which are unidentifiable. ngs_label: Non Docs if a fee code is in a fee schedule for non doc (example, nursing schedule) but the fee code can to mapped to one of the above categories (example, Nurse-immunization), map it to the appropriate category (in this example, 115). If the fee code is billed by a non doc it will be removed from the publication. If it is a non doc and there is not a grouping system category, map to 500.
